subject: Biology
title: ATP: The Energy Currency
description: A micro-lesson that makes ATP: The Energy Currency usable.

hook.text: ATP is a tiny molecule that powers almost every process in every living cell.

buildup.text: When ATP loses a phosphate group, it releases energy. The cell uses that burst to do work.

discovery.text: You produce roughly your own body weight in ATP every single day, recycling it constantly.

twist.text: ATP lasts only seconds before it's broken down and rebuilt. Your body never stockpiles it.

climax.text: From thinking to blinking, from dividing to digesting—ATP fuels all of it.

punchline.text: One molecule runs the whole show.

quiz.question: How much ATP does the human body produce daily?
